title: TTLP() and R2RML_MAKE_QM_FROM_G() generate a graph that can neither be queried from a procedure nor deleted
description:
The RDF graph generated using DB.DBA.TTLP() and DB.DBA.R2RML_MAKE_QM_FROM_G() procedures cannot be deleted and the statement for (sparql ...) do {} with a query over it do not iterate.
Following the example in the page Virtuoso R2RMLSupport I have run the proposed script basic.sql that generates a simple graph from a DB table with single row.
I have used the following code to query the content of the example result graph.
create procedure R2RML.TEST.try() returns integer
{
  for (sparql define input:storage ""
    select ?o
    #from <http://localhost:8890/sparql>
    from <http://example.com/>
    where { ?s ?p ?o } LIMIT 5 ) do
    {
      string_to_file ('trashMe.txt', "o"||'\n', -1);
    };
};
R2RML.TEST.try();


The code snippet does not iterate over the 3 quads of the graph. If another graph is used (like the one commented out in the code) the iterations are executed.
I tried as well to use the dump_one_graph() procedure (actually on other R2RML generated graphs, not the one of this example) and I have noticed that the graphs are not dumped.
I have run the basic.sql example as dba with a Windows Virtuoso Open Source just installed together with the rdb2rdf_dav.vad package.
My VOS version is the following.
Virtuoso Open Source Edition (multi threaded)
Version 7.2.4.3217-threads as of Apr 25 2016
Compiled for Win64 (x86_64-generic-win-64)
I have tried with the following rdb2rdf_dav.vad packages installed, with both the result is the same.
http://opldownload.s3.amazonaws.com/uda/vad-packages/6.3/virtuoso/rdb2rdf_dav.vad
http://s3.amazonaws.com/opldownload/uda/vad-packages/7.0/rdb2rdf_dav.vad
Below is the session with the execution of basic.sql and the CLEAR GRAPH that does not delete the content of the generated graph <http://example.com/>
SQL> load .\basic.sql;
Done. -- 4 msec.
Done. -- 16 msec.
Done. -- 0 msec.
Done. -- 0 msec.
Done. -- 0 msec.
STATE    MESSAGE
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR, value FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Test Item', 10.5);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (2, NULL, 20.0);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (3, 'Another Item', NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL OR name IS NULL;
key_bug_features: RDF graph generated cannot be queried or deleted
